01 — The Shift
Visibility
is easy. Relevance
is hard.
The new equation for brands
↑
Brand noise
↓
Attention

The rules have changed

Brands speak more than ever. People listen less than ever. Attention is saturated. Interruption is ignored. Relevance is earned.

Today, visibility is easy.
Relevance is hard.

Today, brands compete not for shelf space — but for mental space in people's lives.

02 — The New Sport

Sport is no longer just sport

Sport is becoming a way people live, express and connect.

From watching → to doing or participate
Active participation over passive consumption
From performance → to wellbeing
Health, mindset and lifestyle take centre stage
From individual → to belonging
Community becomes the real product
"

Sport is not only consumed anymore. It is lived.

Global Inspiration Session — Havas Play × Decathlon
03 — New Arena

The new competitive set

Sport is no longer competing with sport. The battle is for time, attention and emotional space.

All fighting for the same 24 hours in people's lives
01
Entertainment
Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime
Streaming & binge culture
On-demand content formats
02
Social & Creators
TikTok, Instagram, YouTube
Creator-led communities
Short-form viral content
03
Gaming
Twitch, Discord, esports
Immersive live experiences
Participatory entertainment
The gap —
Brands still talk about products. People are looking for identity, community and belonging.
04 — Strategic Model

Build vs Plug in

Brands don't show up in culture. They build environments inside it — spaces where people participate and connect. The winning model combines both owned systems and borrowed cultural energy.

The ideal is combining both. But effort, resources and time work differently depending on whether you are creating something from scratch or capitalising on something that already exists in culture.

Build communities
Own the space — high investment, high loyalty, long-term compounding returns
Plug into trends
Join what already moves people — faster ROI, lower cost, but limited brand ownership
Create IP
Own formats, events and territories — these define what you stand for in culture
Ride culture
Amplify what resonates — strategic agility, not building from zero
#

Five forces redefining sport as a cultural system.
Each one is a territory. Each one is an opportunity.

Entertainment · Belonging · Identity · Creators · Collaboration
01
Trend 01 of 05

Sport as an entertainment format

The biggest shift is not who plays sport. It's how sport is packaged and consumed.

Format › competition. Narrative › result. Highlights › full matches. Sport is now produced as entertainment.

Kings League
Football reinvented for creators and Twitch — rules designed for entertainment, not tradition
La Velada
Ibai Llanos turned boxing into a creator-culture spectacle watched by millions
Netflix · Amazon · Apple TV+
Streaming platforms acquiring sport rights — sport becomes serialised storytelling
02
Trend 02 of 05

Belonging › Performance

Community is now the product. People don't just practise sport. They join it.

The real retention engine is the community — not the product, not the campaign.

Adidas Runners
City run clubs as a brand ecosystem — community as the product
Rapha Cycling Club
Premium community built around shared ritual and identity
Run Clubs
A global phenomenon — people run for the tribe, not just the miles
03
Trend 03 of 05

Sport as a daily identity code

Sport is part of everyday life — how people move, dress and socialise.

Sport is a social layer of daily life. The brand you wear signals who you are, what you value, and which tribe you belong to.

Salomon Sportstyle
Trail running gear crosses into street fashion — performance as aesthetic
On Running
Performance meets design sensibility — sport as elevated daily uniform
Athleisure · Padel
Sport wear becomes everyday uniform — padel as the new social ritual
04
Trend 04 of 05

Creators redefining sport

Creators are the new entry point. They teach, motivate and build communities.

The first touchpoint with sport is often a creator, not a brand. Creators are the new coaches, the new locker rooms, the new sports media.

Joe Wicks
Workouts as accessible, entertaining content — fitness democratised at scale
Pamela Reif
Fitness community built around aspirational identity — 9M+ followers
Jake Paul
Celebrity × sport × content hybrid — proving creators can own sport narratives
05
Trend 05 of 05

Collaboration is positioning

Brands build relevance through collaboration. It's no longer a tactic — it's a strategic statement.

Campaigns create moments. Collaborations create cultural positioning. What you collaborate on says more about you than any ad.

PSG × Jordan
Football meets basketball culture — a global lifestyle statement beyond sport
Venezia × NOCTA
Serie A football crosses into streetwear via Drake's label
AC Milan × Off-White
Legacy sport institution enters luxury fashion territory
05 — Decathlon
D×PLAY The Opportunity
Become part of people's active lives
∞
Markets
57
Countries

Decathlon is not competing with sports brands

It is competing with how people live sport. The opportunity is to become part of people's active lives — not just their active kit.

From product brand → to culture brand

The gap is closing: brands that define what sport means in culture will own the next decade of relevance.

Relevance = Sport × Culture × Role

What the winning brands do
01
Define the territory
Choose a cultural space and own it completely — not every trend, one clear position.
02
Build the system
Communities, experiences, IP and content that work together over time.
03
Play a role
Stop being a sponsor or advertiser. Become a participant in people's lives.
The brands that will win
Now
Define what sport means in their culture — not just what they sell
System
Build communities, formats and experiences — not campaigns
Role
Become part of people's active identity — not just their active kit
Culture
Design systems that combine building and riding cultural moments
06 — Our Framework

The Havas Play Playbook

From a framework to a brief for a content project. Nine steps to turn a cultural territory into a live, measurable, brand-relevant content system.

01 Contexto y Categoría
02 Objetivos del Contenido
03 Públicos y Comunidades
04 Territorios y Oportunidad
05 Rol y Punto de Vista

07 Estrategia de Contenidos

07 — Systems Thinking

From campaigns to systems

01
Campaigns

Create moments. Spike in visibility. Short-term ROI. Reset after each burst.

02
Communities

Build belonging. Long-term retention. People return because they identify with the group.

03
Experiences

Create participation. People don't just watch — they live the brand in real moments.

04
Systems

Brands build environments inside culture. Spaces where people participate and connect continuously.

"

The brands that will win will define what sport means in culture.
